title: armeria beirana subsp. monchiquensis
intro: Armeria beirana subsp. monchiquensis is a plant species of the genus Armeria, commonly known as the Beirinha or Portuguese thrift. It is a perennial herb native to the island of Madeira, characterized by its pink to white flowers and woody stems. Armeria beirana subsp. monchiquensis has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, and is often prepared as a tea or infusion to harness its medicinal properties.
